mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC over 3-year rolling windows, or housing starts and auto sales diverge without impacting equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase indicates capacity utilization catching up to demand, boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into improved corporate earnings expectations and positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero correlation with 5-day forward returns, or the signal loses significance when GDP revisions exceed 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding the 3 percent threshold forces central banks to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when inflation breaches the 3% threshold, or the signal fails to predict negative returns during supply-driven inflation spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer spending that drive positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined employment and unemployment signal yields a rank IC below 0.01, or positive returns do not materialize when payroll growth outpaces unemployment declines.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals peak capacity utilization and impending demand contraction, triggering risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production declines do not predict negative returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal loses power when inventory adjustments mask underlying demand weakness.
